scope.summary: Implement the pattern-to-rigid-body conversion algorithm (TR-8.1 nine-step sequence), its pre-simulation phase gate (TR-8.2), the Solidify keyframeable blend property (TR-8.3), the tailor_convert_panel_to_trim MCP tool, and the TailorPatternToTrimConverted EventLedger event
acceptance_criteria: Conversion follows the TR-8.1 nine-step order exactly: panel mesh extraction, centroid/inertia computation, tailor_trims row insertion with converted_from_panel_id set, particle inv_mass set to zero, seam constraints on the rigid side converted to GpuTackConstraints, TailorPatternToTrimConverted emitted linking panel_id to trim_id
The source panel geometry is retained as a superseded_panel entry in the CRDT document tree and is NOT deleted per TR-6.1.6
Conversion is rejected (returns InvalidSpec error) if attempted after TailorSimRunStarted has been emitted for the garment per TR-8.2

Solidify property (solidify_blend: f32 in [0,1]) is accepted on any panel in GarmentSpec; at solidify_blend=1.0 all constraint compliances touching the panel are zeroed (kinematic cloth) without full conversion per TR-8.3
Solidify is keyframeable via the animation MaterialFrameParams array (consistent with TR-7.3 for tack_strength)
tailor_convert_panel_to_trim MCP tool accepts garment_id, panel_id, optional trim_name, optional trim_category (armor_plate|accessory default armor_plate) per TR-9 MCP tool listing
Model calling the tool receives a SimulationReceipt-style response with the new trim_id on success
scope.risk_if_missed: MOAT-5 pattern-to-rigid conversion is spec-mandatory (TR-8.1); armor and hard-surface costume authoring is not possible; TailorPatternToTrimConverted event in the EventLedger contract (TR-10.1) has no code path that emits it; the tailor_convert_panel_to_trim MCP tool listed in the spec tool catalog is unimplemented, breaking model-authored garments that reference armor_plate trim_category

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
